overview.description: AI-powered website and web app builder for non-developers and small businesses
overview.description_long: Macaly is an AI-first platform that lets anyone build production-ready websites, web apps, dashboards, and landing pages by describing what they want. The AI agent handles coding, databases, hosting, SEO, analytics, and image generation. Competing with Lovable, Bolt, and v0. Acquired by team.blue in 2025.
overview.tagline: If you can describe it, you can build it

history.origin_story: Founded by second-time founders who previously built Avocode (acquired by Ceros in 2021). Launched on ProductHunt in May 2022, hitting 2nd place with 600+ upvotes and reaching $4K MRR in three weeks.

key_milestones.event: Founded; ProductHunt launch — 2nd place, 600+ upvotes, $4K MRR in 3 weeks
Macaly 2.0 launched — AI-first platform for marketing websites (September)

notable_people.name: Martin Duris
notable_people.note: Co-founder of Avocode (acquired by Ceros in 2021); 500 Startups alumnus

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Macaly is a vibe-coding / AI website builder competing directly with Lovable, Bolt, v0, and ChatGPT. Its core value proposition—generating websites via AI prompts—is rapidly commoditized as foundation models improve and major platforms (Vercel v0, GitHub Copilot, etc.) offer similar capabilities with larger distribution.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: The no-code/AI builder market is real and growing, but intensely competitive with well-funded players (Webflow, Wix AI, Squarespace AI, Bolt, Lovable). Macaly's niche focus on marketing/content sites and the team.blue backing provide some differentiation and European SMB distribution, but the market is crowded.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Acquired by team.blue in 2025, a large European digital services group (formerly known as Combell). Runway and financial stability are no longer at risk due to the parent company's resources.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Post-acquisition stability mitigates funding risk, but the core AI website builder product faces severe commoditization pressure from better-funded competitors and foundation model providers. Long-term differentiation will depend on Macaly's integration with team.blue's hosting/domain ecosystem and European SMB focus.

metadata.notes: Acquired by team.blue in 2025 (announced January 5, 2026). Founded by ex-Avocode team (second-time founders). ProductHunt launch in May 2022 per own blog post. LinkedIn shows ~10 employees. 500 Startups alumnus per Martin Duris LinkedIn profile.
